searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.new(opts: { Compact: boolean?, Placement: string?, Parent: Instance? , Size: UDim2? , Position: UDim2? , Draggable: boolean? }?) → Terminal Construct a terminal. Karet.Start does this for you; call it directly only for a custom setup.

searchDocs.title: toast​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:toast( text: string, opts: { group: string?, title: string?, duration: number? }? ) → () Pop a transient toast notification. Works whether the window is open or closed (the stack lives on the ScreenGui). group keys into the theme like any output group.

searchDocs.title: open​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:open() → () Show the terminal and capture input focus.

searchDocs.title: toggle​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:toggle() → () Open the terminal if closed, close it if open.

searchDocs.title: print​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:print( text: string, group: string? ) → () Print a line to the output log, coloured by a theme group (defaults to "Output"). error, warn, info and success are shorthands for their groups.

searchDocs.title: register​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry:register(def: Definition) → Definition Register a command from a table definition (validated). Returns the stored definition.

searchDocs.title: get​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry:get(name: string) → Definition? Resolve a name or alias to a definition (case-insensitive).

searchDocs.title: list​
searchDocs.content: </> Registry:list() → {string} All registered command names, sorted.

searchDocs.title: Args​
searchDocs.content: </> Context.Args: {[string]: any} The resolved, type-checked positional arguments, keyed by name.

searchDocs.title: Flags​
searchDocs.content: </> Context.Flags: {[string]: boolean} Boolean toggles set with -s / -p.

searchDocs.title: Modifiers​
searchDocs.content: </> Context.Modifiers: {[string]: any} Valued long options: --reason=spam -> ctx.Modifiers.reason.

searchDocs.title: Input​
searchDocs.content: </> Context.Input: any The piped value from the previous command in a pipeline (a | b), or nil.

searchDocs.title: Player​
searchDocs.content: </> Context.Player: Player?  The calling player (nil in Studio / no-player contexts).

searchDocs.title: UI​
searchDocs.content: </> Context.UI: {[string]: any} The rich-output toolkit: ctx.UI.Table{}, ctx.UI.Toast(), ctx.UI.Link(), ... A no-op off the terminal.

searchDocs.title: Print​
searchDocs.content: </> Context:Print(message: any) → () Print a line of normal output. Success, Warn, Err, Info, Hint and Reply are the same but route to their own theme groups.

searchDocs.title: Return​
searchDocs.content: </> Context:Return(...: any) → () Push return values into the pool, matched positionally to the command's Returns schema. These feed / references, pipelines (a | b), and Karet.Run's result. Nils are preserved.

searchDocs.title: Abort​
searchDocs.content: </> Context:Abort(message: string?) → () Cancel the handler mid-run with a message. The command's result becomes { ok = false }, so chains (a && b) see the failure. Unwinds cleanly via a tagged error.

searchDocs.title: Group​
searchDocs.content: </> type Group = string One semantic highlight group. The full set covers surfaces (Background, Surface, Foreground, Muted, Border, Selection), accents (Accent, AccentAlt), syntax (Command, Keyword, String, Number, Comment, Operator, Variable, Type, ...), diagnostics (Error, Warn, Info, Hint, Success) and terminal roles (Prompt, Output, ...). See Theme.Groups for the canonical list.

searchDocs.title: Palette​
searchDocs.content: </> type Palette = {[Group]: Color3 } A map from Group to colour. May be partial when defining a theme (missing groups are derived); Theme.Get always returns a complete one.

searchDocs.title: Define​
searchDocs.content: </> Theme.Define( name: string, palette: Palette ) → Palette Define (or redefine) a named theme from a partial palette. Returns the resolved complete palette, so you can pass it straight to Theme.Set or inspect it. Redefining the active theme recolours live.

searchDocs.title: Set​
searchDocs.content: </> Theme.Set(theme: string | Palette) → () Set the active theme by name or by an explicit (partial) palette. Switching firesTheme.OnChanged, so every subscribed surface recolours at once. Errors if a name isn't defined.

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: </> Theme.Get(group: Group?) → Palette | Color3  Read the active palette, or a single group's colour. Theme.Get() returns the full (always complete) palette; Theme.Get("Error") returns that group's Color3.

searchDocs.title: Name​
searchDocs.content: </> Theme.Name() → string? The name of the active theme, or nil if a raw palette was Theme.Set directly.

searchDocs.title: List​
searchDocs.content: </> Theme.List() → {string} Names of all defined themes.

searchDocs.title: Groups​
searchDocs.content: </> Theme.Groups() → {Group} The canonical list of group names (a copy, so callers can't mutate the source).

searchDocs.title: OnChanged​
searchDocs.content: </> Theme.OnChanged(fn: ( palette: Palette, name: string? ) → ()) → () → () Subscribe to theme changes. The callback fires immediately with the current theme and again on every Theme.Set or redefine. Returns an unsubscribe function. This is how every Karet surface recolours live.

searchDocs.title: Where to go next​
searchDocs.content: Getting started - install and boot KaretWriting commands - register your own typed commandsThe shell - chains, pipes, guards, variables and functionsKaveat - the in-game Luau editor  The full API reference lives under the API tab.

searchDocs.title: Realms​
searchDocs.content: Network decides where the handler runs:  Network	Runs onClient	the client only Server	shipped to the server and run there Shared	the client, with no server round trip Context	a bridge: On.Client commits a payload, On.Server acts on it  Arguments are resolved and type-checked on both realms - the client validates for fast feedback, the server re-validates authoritatively before your handler runs.

searchDocs.title: Args, Flags, Modifiers​
searchDocs.content: Args = { { Name = "target", Types = { "Player" }, Required = true } }, Flags = { { Name = "silent", Short = "s" } }, -- boolean: -s Modifiers = { { Name = "reason", Types = { "String" } } }, -- valued: --reason=spam   Read them in the handler as ctx.Args.target, ctx.Flags.silent, ctx.Modifiers.reason.

searchDocs.title: The context​
searchDocs.content: Every handler gets a Context (ctx). The essentials:  ctx.Args, ctx.Flags, ctx.Modifiers -- resolved input ctx.Print(text) ctx.Success(text) -- themed output ctx.Warn(text) ctx.Err(text) ctx.Return(value) -- push a value to the return pool (and into pipes) ctx.Abort("nope") -- cancel mid-handler ctx.UI.Table{ ... } -- rich output (tables, panels, toasts, links) ctx.Window{ title = "x" } -- an Iris window

searchDocs.title: Builder form​
searchDocs.content: If you prefer a fluent style:  Karet.Command("ping") :Description("pong") :Network("Client") :On("Client", function(ctx) ctx.Print("pong") end) :Register()

searchDocs.title: Permissions​
searchDocs.content: Gate a command by role, or globally:  Karet.Register({ Name = "ban", Permission = "Admin", --[[ ... ]] }) Karet.SetRoleHook(function(player) return isAdmin(player) and "Admin" or "User" end)   See Permissions on the Karet class.

searchDocs.title: Chains​
searchDocs.content: Run commands in sequence and branch on success:  greet A && greet B # run B only if A succeeded greet A || greet B # run B only if A failed greet A & greet B # run both regardless echo one; echo two # ; (or a newline) separates statements

searchDocs.title: Pipes​
searchDocs.content: Thread one command's return value into the next:  echo hello | shout # shout receives "hello" as its first arg + ctx.Input   The piped value auto-fills the next command's first missing required argument, and is also available as ctx.Input.

searchDocs.title: Variables​
searchDocs.content: The @name store. Set, read, and use them in expressions:  set hp 100 echo @hp incr hp 5 (@hp > 50) { notify "healthy" } # a guarded block

searchDocs.title: Guards and type checks​
searchDocs.content: Parenthesised expressions guard a block, and @TYPE[...] checks a value's type:  (x = 5) && (@x == @TYPE[number]) { echo "x is a number" }

searchDocs.title: Functions​
searchDocs.content: Name a sequence of commands and run it like a builtin:  function hi "echo hello; echo there" hi functions # list them unfunction hi   Functions show up in help and completion, work inside chains and pipes, can call each other, and persist with Persist = true.

searchDocs.title: Background jobs​
searchDocs.content: every 5s jump # run a command on a loop jobs # list (click a row to stop it) stop 1 # or: stop all watch health "notify ouch" # run when an expression changes

searchDocs.title: The HUD​
searchDocs.content: Pin a value to an always-on overlay (visible with the terminal closed):  pin health # live value + sparkline pins # list (click to unpin) unpin all

searchDocs.title: Keyboard​
searchDocs.content: Key	DoesTab	accept the autocomplete suggestion Up / Down	history, or move the suggestion Ctrl+R	reverse history search Ctrl+Shift+P	fuzzy command palette Ctrl + / - / 0	zoom

searchDocs.title: Install​
searchDocs.content: Drop the Karet module into ReplicatedStorage (via Rojo, Wally, or the HTTP bootstrap installer), then require it from both a client and a server script.  -- both realms local Karet = require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Karet)

searchDocs.title: Boot it​
searchDocs.content: Call Karet.Start once per realm. The options are the same on both sides; each realm uses the ones that apply to it.  Karet.Start({ Toggle = "Semicolon", -- key (or "LeftControl+Semicolon") to open the terminal Theme = "tokyonight", -- a built-in theme name, or a palette table Placement = "Window", -- Window | Top | Bottom | Middle Kaveat = true, -- enable the Luau editor Persist = true, -- save history/vars/themes/aliases/functions to DataStores Admin = true, -- kick/ban/mute/audit + a dashboard })   See StartConfig for every option.

searchDocs.title: Try it​
searchDocs.content: Open the terminal with your toggle key and type:  help echo hello theme gruvbox neofetch   Press Ctrl+Shift+P for the command palette, or Ctrl+R to search history.

searchDocs.title: Next​
searchDocs.content: Head to Writing commands to add your own.

searchDocs.title: Running code​
searchDocs.content: Kavs run on the server via loadstring (gated for safety - see below). Their print/warn are captured and replayed in the output pane.  Key	DoesCtrl+Enter	run the whole buffer Ctrl+Shift+Enter	run just the selected text

searchDocs.title: Modeline commands​
searchDocs.content: Press : then type a command:  :w save :run run the buffer :q close tab :check lint the buffer :e <f> open file :server server context :ls list kavs :client client context   :w also surfaces any structural problems it finds.

searchDocs.title: Diagnostics​
searchDocs.content: :check (alias :lint) runs a heuristic Luau check and flags the mistakes that bite most: unbalanced brackets, unterminated strings and comments, missing or extra end, and repeat without until, each with a line number. It is a heuristic, not a full parser - it skips strings and comments so keywords inside them don't false-fire.

searchDocs.title: Modules​
searchDocs.content: One kav can require another by name, ModuleScript-style:  -- util.luau return { greet = function(n) return "hi " .. n end } -- main.luau local u = require("util") print(u.greet("bob"))   Modules run once, their return value is cached, and require cycles error cleanly instead of hanging. The .luau suffix is optional. Requiring a real ModuleScript or asset id still works as normal.

searchDocs.title: Enabling server execution​
searchDocs.content: Running arbitrary Luau on the server is gated. Studio is always allowed; on a live game you opt in:  -- server Karet.Start({ KaveatExec = true }) -- or per player: Karet.Start({ KaveatExec = function(player) return player:GetRankInGroup(123) >= 250 end })   warning Enabling KaveatExec on a live game lets allowed clients run arbitrary server code. Gate it to people you trust.

searchDocs.title: StartConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface StartConfig { Toggle: ToggleSpec?-- key / shortcut to open the terminal, e.g. "Semicolon" or "LeftControl+Semicolon" Theme: (string | ThemePalette)?-- a built-in theme name or a palette Placement: ("Window" | "Top" | "Bottom" | "Middle")?-- where the window / bar sits Compact: boolean?-- single-line command bar instead of the full window Draggable: boolean?-- drag the Window-placement terminal by its title bar Parent: Instance? -- where to mount (defaults to PlayerGui) Size: UDim2? -- override the window size Position: UDim2? -- override the window position Kaveat: boolean?-- enable the Kaveat editor PromptType: ("text" | "window")?-- how Dangerous commands confirm: terminal y/N or an Iris modal Persist: boolean?-- save / load the profile via Pik DataStores Admin: boolean?-- enable the moderation suite (kick / ban / mute / audit + dashboard) BuiltIns: boolean?-- register built-in commands (default true) DefaultRole: string?-- server: default role for players RateLimit: RateLimit?-- server: rate limiting (reserved) KaveatExec: (boolean | (player: Player ) → boolean)?-- server: allow kaveat to run code } The options table passed to Karet.Start. The same shape is used on both realms; each realm reads the options that apply to it.

searchDocs.title: Version​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Karet.Version: string The Karet version string, e.g. "0.1.0".

searchDocs.title: RegisterPanel​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.RegisterPanel(panel: Panel) → Panel Register a dockable panel, a named view usually built with ctx.Window inside its Open. Panels are surfaced by the panels command and opened with open <name>.

searchDocs.title: RegisterExtension​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.RegisterExtension(ext: Extension) → Extension Register a whole extension: its Commands and Types are wired into Karet, its Panels become openable, and Init(Karet) runs once. This is the unit that makes Karet a platform.

searchDocs.title: Panels​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.Panels() → {Panel} Every registered panel.

searchDocs.title: SetVar​
searchDocs.content: Variables </> Karet.SetVar( name: string, value: any ) → () Set a shell variable. The same value is readable in the shell as @name, since this writes straight into the evaluator's scope (Karet.Env). Seed defaults before Karet.Start.

searchDocs.title: GetVar​
searchDocs.content: Variables </> Karet.GetVar(name: string) → any Read a shell variable (the @name store).

searchDocs.title: Run​
searchDocs.content: Output </> Karet.Run(line: string) → any Run a line through the shell (alias-expanded), exactly as if it had been typed. Returns the chain result { ok, returns?, error? }, or nil before Karet.Start has run.

searchDocs.title: Toast​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client Output </> Karet.Toast( text: string, opts: { group: string?, title: string?, duration: number? }? ) → () Pop a transient toast notification on the active terminal. No-op on the server or before a terminal exists. group keys into the theme like any output group. Handy for extensions and admin events.

searchDocs.title: UseProfile​
searchDocs.content: Setup </> Karet.UseProfile(name: string) → Profile Switch the active profile (history, theme, saved kavs, aliases, functions), creating it if it doesn't exist yet. Karet.Profile then points at it.

searchDocs.title: Register​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.Register(def: CommandDefinition) → CommandDefinition Register a command from a table definition. See the commands guide for the full shape (Args, Flags, Modifiers, On, Network, Permission).

searchDocs.title: Command​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.Command(name: string) → CommandBuilder Start the fluent builder: Karet.Command("ban"):Alias("b"):Network("Server"):On(...):Register().

searchDocs.title: DefineType​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.DefineType( name: string, def: TypeDefinition ) → () Register a custom argument type (a la Cmdr). Types parse and validate the raw token into a typed value, and can supply autocomplete.

searchDocs.title: List​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.List() → {string} The names of every registered command.

searchDocs.title: Get​
searchDocs.content: Commands </> Karet.Get(name: string) → CommandDefinition? A single command's definition, or nil.

searchDocs.title: SetRoleHook​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Permissions </> Karet.SetRoleHook(fn: (player: Player ) → string) → () Decide a player's role. Roles are compared against a command's Permission using the rank ordering from Karet.SetRoles.

searchDocs.title: SetAuthHook​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Permissions </> Karet.SetAuthHook(fn: ( player: Player , command: string ) → boolean?) → () A per-command gate. Return true to allow, false to deny, or nil to fall through to the normal role check.

searchDocs.title: SetGroupHook​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Permissions </> Karet.SetGroupHook(fn: (player: Player ) → number) → () Resolve a player's numeric rank (e.g. a Roblox group rank), for commands that compare against a number.

searchDocs.title: AllowPlayer​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Permissions </> Karet.AllowPlayer(userId: number) → () Allowlist a userId outright.

searchDocs.title: DenyPlayer​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Permissions </> Karet.DenyPlayer(userId: number) → () Denylist a userId outright.

searchDocs.title: SetDefaultRole​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Permissions </> Karet.SetDefaultRole(role: string) → () The role players get when no role hook is set or it returns nothing.

searchDocs.title: SetRoles​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Permissions </> Karet.SetRoles(ranks: {[string]: number}) → () Define the role -> rank ordering used for Permission comparisons, e.g. { User = 0, Mod = 50, Admin = 100 }.

searchDocs.title: PreExecute​
searchDocs.content: Middleware </> Karet.PreExecute(fn: (ctx: Context) → any?) → () Run before every command. Return false to cancel it.

searchDocs.title: PostExecute​
searchDocs.content: Middleware </> Karet.PostExecute(fn: ( ctx: Context, result: any ) → ()) → () Run after every command with its result.

searchDocs.title: OnErr​
searchDocs.content: Middleware </> Karet.OnErr(fn: ( ctx: Context, err: any ) → ()) → () Run when a command errors.

searchDocs.title: OnConnect​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Middleware </> Karet.OnConnect(fn: (player: Player ) → ()) → () Run when a player joins.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: Setup </> Karet.Start(opts: StartConfig?) → Terminal? Boot Karet. On the client this builds and wires the terminal and the command evaluator and returns the Terminal; on the server it stands up the networking listener and the auth pipeline and returns nil. The same call works in both realms. Karet.Start({ Toggle = "Semicolon", Theme = "tokyonight", Kaveat = true })
